task_name: Workspace-scoped board, metrics, activity, and card APIs

concerns_addressed: Cycle-1 move body key mismatch RESOLVED: client api.ts:83-91 sends body containing `index`, BoardPage.tsx:252-256 passes `index`, and server routes.ts:971-973 destructures and validates `index`. Chain agrees end-to-end.

Test asserts body shape: api.test.ts:121-123 parses the move request body and asserts `toEqual({ toColumnId: 2, index: 3, version: 3 })` plus `not.toHaveProperty('position')` — a real regression guard, not just a path check.

strengths: All board/columns/cards/metrics/activity/presence routes are workspace-scoped: every route parses workspaceId, performs a `lookupMembership` 404 guard, and constrains queries by `workspace_id` (e.g., routes.ts:682-694, 1099-1124, 1194-1211).

Optimistic locking (routes.ts:993-998, 906-924), WIP enforcement (routes.ts:862-869, 1022-1033), activity recording with workspace_id (recordActivity, routes.ts:647-672), and soft-delete (routes.ts:946-950) all preserved and scoped.
Non-member card reads return 404 via the testable createScopedBoardService factory (routes.ts:123-159), avoiding workspace-existence leakage.
core/* is untouched: metrics/WIP helpers receive only pre-scoped rows; no changes to server/src/core/ in the task range.
Client board APIs require an explicit workspaceId argument on every method (api.ts:48-133); no slug-based routing introduced.

Move-route mutating UPDATEs (routes.ts:1045, 1054) operate on rows already SELECT...FOR UPDATE'd with `workspace_id = $2` in the same transaction (routes.ts:983-986, 1014-1019), so they are transactionally workspace-scoped and locked.

issues.description: Defense-in-depth: the two move UPDATEs filter by `id` alone rather than also including `workspace_id`. Safe today because the card and siblings are FOR UPDATE-verified within the workspace in the same transaction, but an inline `AND workspace_id = $N` would make the guarantee local to each statement.

DELETE /workspaces/:workspaceId/presence omits the `lookupMembership` 404 guard present on every other scoped route. Harmless because it only clears the caller's own presence key (req.user!.id), but it is inconsistent with the surrounding pattern.
